name: AppImage
description: Universal software package format for Linux — single-file executables that run on any distribution.

seo.title: AppImage — CI/CD for Applications
seo.meta_description: AppImage: Universal software package format for Linux — single-file executables that run on any distribution.

when_to_use: Ship portable Linux apps without system dependencies
Distribute desktop tools that run on any distro
Single-file CI artifact for Linux application releases
Enable zero-install app trials on user machines

pros: Single-file binary runs on any recent Linux distro
No install step, no root, no sandbox daemon
Simple for end users: download and execute
cons: No automatic updates without extra tooling
No real sandboxing unlike Flatpak or Snap
Integration with system menus is clunky
review.verdict: The simplest way to ship a Linux desktop app, at the cost of security and updates.
review.best_for: Indie developers shipping cross-distro Linux GUI apps with minimal tooling.
review.not_for: Apps that need sandboxing, auto-updates, or curated distribution.
